channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This messaging mecca integrates Slack, WhatsApp, and Telegram
item.description: I like chatting with my friends—who doesn’t?—but I don’t always know where to find them. There are simply too many apps. Some of my friends text, others use WhatsApp, while others yet insist on using Discord or the DM feature in whatever random social network they prefer.
item.dc:creator: Justin Pot, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla's self-driving cars under fire again
item.description: Tesla's Full Self-Driving system faces federal investigation following 58 reports of crashes, with six vehicles running red lights before colliding with other cars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Claude just got a skill upgrade and it’s coming for your workflows
item.description: Model builders are moving beyond simple AI chatbots to creating comprehensive assistants that, in the words of AI dignitary Ethan Mollick, “do real work” in enterprise workflows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hi-fi legend Quad's new super-powerful, super-connected stereo amp could be all the amp you need for the rest of your life
item.description: Money No Object: Quad launches its "finest-ever" integrated amp, and it's future-proofed in a ton of ways
item.dc:creator: Carrie Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: Meta adding AI chatbot safety features for teens
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Instagram and Facebook, plans to roll out new safety features for its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Toonstar’s new ‘Uncle Roger’ cartoon embraces AI—but slop it’s not
item.description: “The gyoza needs to look a little whiter. It’s too pink.” Nigel Ng is genially micromanaging the look and feel of Fried, an animated series that will premiere on YouTube later this year.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: From heart health to health equity, Dr. David Goff is shaping science and inspiring public service
item.description: "We've had a lot of success, but we're now having backsliding, and there's a lot of work to be done," said Dr. David Goff. The post From heart health to health equity, Dr. David Goff is shaping science and inspiring public service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Uber wants to become the ‘Uber’ of data labeling
item.description: Following its entrance into the data labeling space last year with the launch of a new division, Scaled Solutions, Uber on Thursday introduced a pilot project in the US market that will see its drivers, like their counterparts in India, annotate data for use in AI training.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Getac launches world's fastest rugged laptop - and, for the first time in decades, we have an AMD-powered rugged notebook
item.description: Getac’s S510AD introduces AMD’s AI power to rugged laptops, blending durability, sustainability, and field-ready performance for professional users.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Apple adds game-changing iPhone document feature that users waited years to see
item.description: Apple launches iOS 26 with new Preview app that combines document editing, PDF annotation and scanning features into one streamlined iPhone experience.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft: Russia, China increasingly using AI to escalate cyberattacks on the US
item.description: Russia, China, Iran and North Korea are using artificial intelligence to improve their cyberattacks on U.S. companies, governments and individuals, according to new research from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Microsoft set to escape French antitrust probe as Qwant complaint falters
item.description: Microsoft is expected to sidestep a French antitrust probe into its search business after regulators signaled plans to dismiss a complaint by local rival Qwant, easing scrutiny of the US tech giant even as Europe ramps up enforcement of digital competition rules.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Oracle’s big bet for AI: Zettascale10
item.description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is not just going all-in on AI, but on AI at incredible scale. This week, the company announced what it calls the largest AI supercomputer in the cloud, OCI Zettascale10.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Got a Pixel Fold
item.description: When you live with a folding phone and you’re the kind of person who likes taking total control of your Android environment, you’re bound to realize something pretty quickly: The apps that work brilliantly in a standard Android setup don’t always work so well when you’re dealing with a dual display.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
